suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update rust-library-worker guidance so Windows workers do not rely on a POSIX-only init path and do not assume the installed GitNexus CLI exposes detect_changes exactly as documented.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviews reported that worker sessions could not follow the literal procedure because .factory/init.sh is POSIX-shell-oriented on Windows and the available GitNexus CLI lacked the documented detect_changes command.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify Windows startup guidance for library-only missions so workers can skip .factory/init.sh when baseline Cargo validation already proves setup is healthy and no services are required.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The resolver-store-boundary-proof worker noted `.factory/init.sh` was unnecessary on this Windows library mission because baseline workspace tests already passed without extra setup.

rejectedObservations.observation: Store stale-GitNexus handling for test-only review work in shared library knowledge.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected as too procedural and reviewer-specific for library knowledge; better handled in worker skill guidance
